programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The National
programme.desc: CBC's flagship nightly news program, featuring the day's top stories with in-depth and original journalism; Adrienne Arsenault and Ian Hanomansing anchor.

programme.title: This Week's Top Stories
programme.desc: Making sense of the big stories that made news across Canada and around the world from this past week.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: About That
programme.desc: Andrew Chang expands the understanding of the stories everybody's talking about.

programme.title: The Fifth Estate
programme.desc: The top-notch team of hosts Bob McKeown, Gillian Findlay, Mark Kelley and Habiba Nosheen provides unique and engaging documentaries from Canada and beyond. "The Fifth Estate" is a reference to the term Fourth Estate, and was chosen to highlight the program's determination to go beyond everyday news into original journalism. The series has won numerous awards for its reporting, including Gemini Awards, International Emmys, the Michener Award, and even an Academy Award for a 1981 episode that was released in theaters the following year. Since September 1975, Canadians have tuned in to experience interesting stories that are always well-crafted and thought-provoking.

programme.title: CTV National News with Sandie Rinaldo

programme.desc: Stories that showcase what makes Canada unique.

programme.desc: Top stories from coast-to-coast as reported by CTV News local journalists across Canada.

programme.title: W5
programme.desc: W5 investigates a three-decades-old cold case - the unsolved murder of a young boy.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer spices things up as he makes pepper-inspired dishes with catfish and clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer dresses sablefish in a mole sauce that has a hint of maple and chocolate; lobster has its sweet side.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up fast faves using scallops and trout, all done in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts mixes the sweet flavors of fruit with fish as he makes dishes with salmon and scallops.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates some dairy-infused dishes using red snapper and scampi.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts gets the party started with mouth-watering grilled recipes!

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts kicks things up a notch with four spicy recipes on the grill.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts explores the barbeque culture of Spain with classic recipes.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts butters up with a menu featuring some of his favourite recipes.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts gets creative on the grill with salt.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows his sweet side with four decadent desserts.

programme.title: Watts on the Grill
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts top tips for achieving smoky flavours.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: How vinegar can bring out the flavors of walleye, lobster and crawfish.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Cocktails can be inspiration for cooking great seafood.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: How to prepare cold dishes with arctic char and shrimp.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Have fun wrapping seafood to create delicious dishes with swordfish and shrimp.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer Watts cooks up tasty haddock and escargot treats using garlic.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how much flavor seeds can add to sole and squid.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer spices things up as he makes pepper-inspired dishes with catfish and clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Spencer dresses sablefish in a mole sauce that has a hint of maple and chocolate; lobster has its sweet side.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up fast faves using scallops and trout, all done in under 30 minutes.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts mixes the sweet flavors of fruit with fish as he makes dishes with salmon and scallops.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates some dairy-infused dishes using red snapper and scampi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: It’s all about street food in this Vietnamese episode.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Mary Tang cooks up Cantonese recipes perfect for days when you need comfort food.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Fun host Lisa Nguyen shows us just how versatile noodles are.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Lisa Nguyen shows us recipes from Vietnam's capital city of Hanoi.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Home cook Mary Tang shows us popular Hong Kong culinary delights.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us how to celebrate the Moon Festival.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang cooks up traditional Cantonese dim sum recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Mary Tang shows us four easy Cantonese seafood recipes.

programme.title: One World Kitchen
programme.sub-title: Vietnamese Food With a French Twist
programme.desc: Vietnamese dishes with French influence.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs take a shot at dumplings, from classic Americana to upscale European!

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs go raw – and raw vegan – with their variations on a classic Japanese dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs offer worldwide inspirations on a classic rice dish.

programme.title: Up The Dish
programme.desc: Up the Dish's four chefs begin in East Asia with classic Pad Thai then make twists inspired by the classic.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Easy Weeknight Recipes for Busy Days
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco whips up four delicious multi-cooker dishes.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Fast and Flavourful Dinners for Monday to Thursday
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco has your weeknight dinners covered with delicious recipes.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Weeknight Dishes made in Little Thyme
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancecesco has four recipes to make your life easy including Apple Butter Pork Chops and Rissotto.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.sub-title: Sweet, Salty, and Surprising Multi-Cooker Recipes
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up a week’s worth of multi-cooker meals including Chicken Marbella and a decadent Chocolate Cake.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up a Peachy Pulled Pork, a velvety Key Lime Pie and other recipes in her multi-cooker.

programme.title: Pressure's On
programme.desc: Host Vanessa Gianfrancesco cooks up a Vegetarian Chili, Salted Caramel Cheesecake and other recipes in her multi-cooker.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts creates recipes that combine sour and tart flavors with grouper clams.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts demonstrates how a little caffeinated flavour can bring your fish dish to the next level.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how you can blend meat and seafood flavours to create tantalizing surf and turf dishes.

programme.title: Fish The Dish
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts shows how cooking with whole fish and shellfish is an opportunity for fantastic flavour.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a delicious family-style roast beef dinner in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Fish and chips are always great for a quick, inexpensive family dinner.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Pizza night is quick and budget-friendly as Chef Spencer Watts offers up his favourite recipes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts makes a full, family-style Indian meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Why order take-out when you can make it fresher, faster, and even healthier at home?

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: It’s all about lasagna with Chef Spencer Watts’ delicious and nutritious 30-minute meal.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a quick and nutritious chicken bake meal for four.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: The best way to do a fast, inexpensive dinner for four? Make breakfast for dinner.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts cooks up a filling vegetarian meal in around 30 minutes.

programme.title: Spencer's Big 30
programme.desc: Chef Spencer Watts throws a fun and fresh fiesta for a delicious taco night.

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: When We Were Apollo
programme.desc: Take an intimate and personal look at Apollo through the lives and experiences of its most inspiring figures.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Hubble: Eye in the Sky
programme.desc: In this beautiful show, viewers get an inside look at the challenges of operating the Hubble telescope.

programme.title: The Saturn V Story
programme.desc: This is the story of the most powerful machine ever built and a nation who watched on with hope, together.

programme.title: The Jupiter Enigma
programme.desc: NASA’s Juno mission to Jupiter is revealing the origins of our solar system, when Earth's fate was uncertain.

programme.title: Faster Than Light: the Dream of Interstellar Flight
programme.desc: Next-generation rocket technology is fueling the dream of traveling across the void.

programme.title: Powered by Space: Reinventing Space Flight
programme.desc: Scientists are building a new type of rocket engine using plasma to revolutionize space travel.

programme.title: China's Long March Into Space
programme.desc: Three men sit atop 500 tons of liquid fuel in China's Shenzhou 7 ship, ready to blast 185 miles into space.

programme.title: Voyage of Curiosity
programme.desc: Curiosity's robot rover exploring Mars is now known as one of the greatest achievements in space exploration.

programme.title: Space Colonies
programme.desc: Space colonization may start with the Moon. Getting material there may be relatively simple. But risks exist.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode explores the search for hurricane scientists' holy grail.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: This episode looks at how plants and animals cope with the annual onslaught in the hurricane zone.

programme.title: Hurricane: The Anatomy
programme.desc: With coastal habitats and natural defenses irretrievably damaged, the future appears bleak.

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.desc: While nature bestowed Earth with life, it enveloped deadly Venus in sulfuric acid clouds and volcanic flows.

programme.title: Atlas of a Changing Earth
programme.desc: This documentary reveals the processes melting glaciers, and explores how rising seas may threaten coastlines.

programme.title: Shockwave
programme.desc: A powerful earthquake and tsunami will some day devastate the Pacific Northwest. What can we do to prepare?

programme.title: When We Were Apollo
programme.desc: Take an intimate and personal look at Apollo through the lives and experiences of its most inspiring figures.

programme.title: Hubble's Enduring Legacy
programme.desc: The Hubble's iconic images are brought to life to answer the most important questions now facing astronomers.

programme.title: Earth in 1000 years
programme.desc: Earth's stores of ice on Greenland and Antarctica are melting, and sea levels rising. Where is Earth headed?

programme.title: Cosmic Journeys
programme.sub-title: The Largest Black Holes in the Universe
programme.desc: Black holes are mysterious. Find out what impact the massive giants have had on the Universe and its galaxies.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: Join experts as they launch a series of adventures across land and sea to get up close with apex predators.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: Host Kina Scollay takes an expedition boat into the heart of the wilderness.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In this episode, Kina invites the viewer into his world of researching and filming great white sharks.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In cold, remote waters, sea lions perform an underwater ballet full of grace and brute strength.

programme.title: Life is One: Growing Up Sun Bear
programme.desc: This is the story of three sun bears from Indonesia and their perilous journey from captivity into the wild.

programme.title: The Lembeh Strait Walking Fish
programme.desc: The Lembeh Strait was emptied by overfishing. Now a new community of unusual creatures calls it home.

programme.title: The Dark Side of the Ocean
programme.desc: One man plans to do what no man has done before. His aim, to unlock the secrets of the dark ocean.

programme.title: Secret World of Mollusks
programme.desc: Mollusks deserve a second chance to better the first impression, since their world is truly one of a kind.

programme.title: Coral: The Heart of the Ocean
programme.desc: This Phillippines marine park is a model for sustainable development and a true laboratory of the future.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Off the coast of the Philippines are some of the most amazing sea creatures in the world.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: With 17,000 islands. the Indonesia Archipelago is one of the most biodiverse places on earth.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Japan's unique coastlines are home to some of the world's most diverse marine ecosystems.

programme.title: Ocean Wild
programme.desc: Thai marine wildlife is like no other. In this episode, discover how these creatures evolved.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: Join experts as they launch a series of adventures across land and sea to get up close with apex predators.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: Host Kina Scollay takes an expedition boat into the heart of the wilderness.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In this episode, Kina invites the viewer into his world of researching and filming great white sharks.

programme.title: Ocean Predators
programme.desc: In cold, remote waters, sea lions perform an underwater ballet full of grace and brute strength.

programme.title: Life is One: Growing Up Sun Bear
programme.desc: This is the story of three sun bears from Indonesia and their perilous journey from captivity into the wild.

programme.title: The Lembeh Strait Walking Fish
programme.desc: The Lembeh Strait was emptied by overfishing. Now a new community of unusual creatures calls it home.

programme.title: The Dark Side of the Ocean
programme.desc: One man plans to do what no man has done before. His aim, to unlock the secrets of the dark ocean.

programme.title: Secret World of Mollusks
programme.desc: Mollusks deserve a second chance to better the first impression, since their world is truly one of a kind.

programme.title: Coral: The Heart of the Ocean
programme.desc: This Phillippines marine park is a model for sustainable development and a true laboratory of the future.

programme.title: The Nature Effect
programme.desc: Experiencing nature stimulates unexpected biological and psychological resources, heightening your well-being.

programme.title: Our Roots, Our Future
programme.desc: Scientists are studying root growth. Roots may help solve climate change and protect the environment.

programme.title: In the Mind of Plants
programme.desc: Can plants sense pain? Some scientists are convinced plants have surpassed animals on their evolutionary path.

programme.title: Chasing the Giant Croc
programme.desc: On the Island of Mindanao lurks a man-eating monster. Learn the story of the largest crocodile ever captured!

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Evolution Earth
programme.desc: From the earth to the stars to the edge of the unknown, explore the mystery, majesty and power of the world we live in and beyond.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The highest mountain range in Portugal is the Serra da Estrela. The episode embarks on a voyage of discovery to adventurous rock formations and villages steeped in tradition. The nature park is the largest protected area on the Iberian peninsula. In the mountain range we accompany the young scientist Ana Matos, who wants to breathe new life into old traditions, and cheese maker Maria Natália Lopes. She watches over more than 350 sheeps.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.sub-title: South West Portugal: Alentejano and Costa Vicentina
programme.desc: In the southwest of Portugal lies the little known Parque Natural do Sudoeste Alentejano e Costa Vicentina. It encompasses a narrow coastal strip that runs along the coast to the south of Portugal. Sparsely populated, this coastal region, which was placed under protection in 1988, is considered one of the last intact ecosystems in Europe. The steep cliffs, the almost constant wind and high waves have ensured that mass tourism has not gained a foothold here. There are still near-natural forests and a diverse bird life.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: Madeira - Portugal's autonomous island in the Atlantic Ocean, is closer to the African coast than to the mother country Portugal, from which it is separated by about 900 kilometers. The island's name comes from Portuguese sailors who claimed the uninhabited isle for themselves in 1419. Madeira means wood and that was available in abundance. Almost the entire surface was covered by dense forest. Since 1982, two-thirds of the landscape has been protected.

programme.title: Portugal's Nature Parks
programme.desc: The "Parque Natural da Ria Formosa" is one of the largest lagoon conservation areas in Europe. Bordered by five natural islands. A mosaic of sandbanks, brackish and freshwater lagoons and salt marshes. In 2010, the "Ria Formosa" was chosen as one of the seven natural wonders of Portugal. It is a unique ecosystem: in the winter months, more than 20,000 birds gather here. In addition, the nature park has one of the largest seahorse populations internationally. More than half of all fish species in the Atlantic are said to spawn in the lagoon.

programme.title: Over the Islands of Africa
programme.sub-title: Over the Islands of Africa, Zanzibar
programme.desc: Photographer Matthias Ziegler is an Africa specialist. He has visited nearly every country on the continent on assignments for international agencies. Zanzibar, however, is new territory for him. He's come to the former slave island for a photo book – and this time he's not alone. To see the Tanzanian archipelago from the air, he has enlisted the services of ultralight pilot Richard Meredith-Hardy of England to fly him around in his FIB, or flying inflatable boat. Perfect conditions for an adventurous journey: as a world champion ultralight pilot, Richard and his FIB can take off and land practically anywhere – in theory.

programme.title: Wild Pacific - The Beauty of Life
programme.desc: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions. It extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean.

programme.title: Secrets of the North Sea
programme.desc: The North Sea is enigmatic. It presents itself in cool shades of grey. Yet who would have guessed that beneath the chilly waves are opulent coral gardens, courting squids, and several of the largest sea creatures on the planet?

programme.title: Mysterious Oceanic Oases - Hotspots of Life
programme.desc: Countless marine animals frolic on the steep slopes of underwater volcanoes. In many cases, several are rare species not found elsewhere. This is in part due to the fact that plankton clings to the wall and provides the fish with food in abundance. This documentary shows several of these oases and also examines the impact dive tourism has and whether preventive measures can help to maintain them. Nature and underwater filmmaker Rolf Möltgen undertakes dives into the mysterious underwater mountain landscapes with marine scientists from all corners of the globe. This journey takes us to Mexico, to snappers, white-tip reef sharks, Galapagos sharks and giant mantas. We visit the Maldives, Colombia and Costa Rica, where the team documents rare species such as deep sea burbots, small tooth sand tiger and dogfish sharks, in depths of more than 3 metres. The eerie underwater mountain world proves: everyone plays a part in the network of life. Thanks to consistent protective regulations, it has been possible to retain the underwater diversity - especially in the unique oases of the high seas

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: The contours of the Mediterranean have changed during the course of its history. Near Gibraltar, Africa was once connected to the European continent. When the land bridge at Gibraltar collapsed, the Mediterranean basin filled up again. The Suez Canal is today connected to the Red Sea. This route enables animals to reach the Mediterranean, where they cause problems.

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Rugged rocks, blue sea and idyllic, sandy beaches - this is the Mediterranean as we know it. But this impression is deceptive: the Mediterranean is under constant change. The continents are moving towards one another. Animals, that have to survive under such extreme conditions, must find a way to escape the dangers. Between deserts and volcanoes, the challenges for the animals are enormous.

programme.title: Europe's far North - Lofoten and the Arctic Ocean
programme.desc: Lofoten is the rugged archipelago in the Norwegian Arctic Ocean. Whoever arrives here is really at the end. Moskenes is the end of the line, and anyone who wants to go further needs a boat or a plane. The only way to get to the open sea is via the Maelstrom, which is quite dangerous. On the outermost headland, there are only a few ruins left. The last inhabitants are long gone, a supply was hardly possible. But fishermen are on the way, mainly to catch cod. And there is the coastal administration, which takes care of broken sea marks and lighthouses. A visit to one of the luxurious ships of the Hurtig Line is not to be missed. They shuttle off rough coasts on an eleven-day trip between the Russian border and southern Norway. A floating workplace for a wide variety of professions. The island of Andøya is not served by the Hurtiglinie. It lies too far out in the Atlantic. One of the most important observatories in Europe is located here. A young woman, Sandra Blindheim, is the boss. She is responsible for the large laser that delivers important information to scientists around the world. On clear winter nights, there is a wonderful panoramic view of the aurora borealis.

programme.title: Jacques Cousteau's Legacy - Return To The Undersea World
programme.desc: A composition of wildlife footage, archive material and elaborate re-enactments results in a brilliant reconstruction of the life of famed marine biologist and environmental pioneer Jacques Cousteau. On the trail of the most famous oceanographer of the 20th Century, the team spent two years filming original locations on three different continents, in four different oceans. The film charts how Cousteau’s outlook on the environment changed throughout his many decades at sea around the globe. On his personal journey to enlightenment, he changed from a man who killed sharks in hate and thoughtlessly blew up coral reefs, to a radical environmentalist.

programme.title: Realm of the robber - Christmas Island
programme.desc: Thievery and treachery are second nature for the world's largest land crab. This documentary explores the untold story of Christmas Island's iconic robber crabs. Armed with claws that deliver more force than a lion's bite, they can grow a metre across and are relentless in their search for food. However, the brash crustaceans have surprisingly complex private lives and often find themselves in precarious situations, whether escaping from frenzied ants or risking their life to deposit fertilised eggs into a raging ocean. To tell the story of the robber crab's life cycle, directors Moritz Katz and Braydon Moloney spent a year in the field, following their subjects in minute detail. The tragedies, trials and triumphs paint a fresh, humorous portrait of Christmas Island and its nefarious denizens.

programme.title: Lobsters - The Knights Of The Deep
programme.desc: The knights of the deep are on the move; almost no part of their bodies is without armor. Like medieval warriors, these fighters are well armed – not with sword and shield, but with scissors and forceps and defensive armor that is covered with spikes and hooks. And even though they are well protected and have an uncanny awareness of their surroundings, many of these proud knights will face a gloomy destiny. Many of them will end upon a plate. Gourmets all over the world are in love with these famous crustaceans: lobsters! For marine biologists lobsters are similarly fascinating: as research objects. We are on their trail in Cape Breton, an island in the northern Atlantic and part of the Canadian province Nova Scotia, where one of the largest populations of lobsters has its home.

programme.title: Vicious Beauties - The secret World of the Jelly Fish
programme.desc: Watching the graceful movement of these gelatinous animals is almost hypnotic. Although their bodies are 98% water, jellyfish are deceptively clever and dangerous. But where do these primordial creatures come from? How do they reproduce and how long do they live?

programme.title: Predators of a Different Kind - In the World of the Unknown Sharks
programme.desc: With the aid of modern technology, reveal the unknown behaviour of some unusual species of shark: lemon sharks and their white-tip reef counterparts. Our film trip begins on the Bahamas. To be more precise: in the turquoise-blue waters of the Grand Bahama Bank, in an exactly fixed location. In the spring, 70 – 80 pregnant lemon sharks arrive here. This huge shark population was first discovered just a few years ago by shark researcher, Professor Sam Gruber. Many of the females are tired and rest on the seabed. Pregnant tiger sharks swim in the midst of this group. Without any protection whatsoever, our cameramen shoot their footage, surrounded by sharks and succeed in capturing images hitherto unseen. We continue our journey to Gainesville, Florida, to meet Gordon Hubbell, the leading shark denture expert with the world’s largest shark denture collection. He knows everything about the evolutionary history of the lemon sharks, in addition to those of the white-tip reef variety off Cocos Island, Costa Rica. Here, we encounter large schools of fish, unprecedented numbers of stingrays, as well as hammerhead sharks. However, it is the white-tip reek sharks that make the biggest impression. Their performance begins late at night. Marauding, they patrol in large groups through the reefs and hunt everything that moves. Scales and dead prey fish float above the reef – a welcoming change of diet for the ubiquitous barracudas.

programme.title: Giants of Fortune - The Whale Sharks of Oslob
programme.desc: In the little Filipino village of Oslob on the Cebu Island, people suffer from typhoons and overfishing. Until the world’s biggest fish and the Internet change everything.Fisherman Zosimo’s dearest wish is for his children to graduate from high school one day. Yet the chance that this wish will come true is tiny, since at times Zosimo doesn’t even manage to feed the family. Typhoons and overfishing make the lives of Oslob fishermen difficult.

programme.title: The Oceans Seven - The Marathon Swimming Challenge
programme.desc: André Wiersig, born in 1972, has achieved what no German has ever done before: he has swum through the world's seven most famous straits, the so-called Ocean's Seven. To prepare for it, he chose a familiar but hostile environment. In his carport, he hardened himself in a rain barrel filled with groundwater.

programme.title: Symphony of the Seas - Underwater Wonders of Australia's Ocean
programme.desc: Our journeys take us all around the world from Patagonia to the USA, from Greenland to South Africa and from Cambodia to Australia and leads us to the most fascinating places where landscapes animals and humans are the main focus of our documentaries. Whether on land or at sea – the last paradises of the world are our destination.

programme.title: The Last Paradises: Patagonia - Coast of the Killer Whale
programme.desc: On the wild coast of Patagonia, the nature calendar describes the most diverse visiting times of fascinating animals, from the southern right whale and other large whales, via the sea lions and elephant seals up to the orcas and millions of penguins. The encounters with whales and their babies are spectacular. Hardly anywhere else on earth can one experience both the brutal and the delightful sides of nature so vividly and so close. The trip to the hot spots for animal watchers and researchers leads us inter alia to the Caleta Valdez. This is where, during the mating season, the southern elephant seals bring their babies into the world, mate and enjoy a little sunbathing on the beach.

programme.title: Wild Pacific - The Beauty of Life
programme.desc: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions. It extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Southern Ocean.

programme.title: Secrets of the North Sea
programme.desc: The North Sea is enigmatic. It presents itself in cool shades of grey. Yet who would have guessed that beneath the chilly waves are opulent coral gardens, courting squids, and several of the largest sea creatures on the planet?

programme.title: Mysterious Oceanic Oases - Hotspots of Life
programme.desc: Countless marine animals frolic on the steep slopes of underwater volcanoes. In many cases, several are rare species not found elsewhere. This is in part due to the fact that plankton clings to the wall and provides the fish with food in abundance. This documentary shows several of these oases and also examines the impact dive tourism has and whether preventive measures can help to maintain them. Nature and underwater filmmaker Rolf Möltgen undertakes dives into the mysterious underwater mountain landscapes with marine scientists from all corners of the globe. This journey takes us to Mexico, to snappers, white-tip reef sharks, Galapagos sharks and giant mantas. We visit the Maldives, Colombia and Costa Rica, where the team documents rare species such as deep sea burbots, small tooth sand tiger and dogfish sharks, in depths of more than 3 metres. The eerie underwater mountain world proves: everyone plays a part in the network of life. Thanks to consistent protective regulations, it has been possible to retain the underwater diversity - especially in the unique oases of the high seas

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: The contours of the Mediterranean have changed during the course of its history. Near Gibraltar, Africa was once connected to the European continent. When the land bridge at Gibraltar collapsed, the Mediterranean basin filled up again. The Suez Canal is today connected to the Red Sea. This route enables animals to reach the Mediterranean, where they cause problems.

programme.title: Secrets of the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Rugged rocks, blue sea and idyllic, sandy beaches - this is the Mediterranean as we know it. But this impression is deceptive: the Mediterranean is under constant change. The continents are moving towards one another. Animals, that have to survive under such extreme conditions, must find a way to escape the dangers. Between deserts and volcanoes, the challenges for the animals are enormous.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: 320 million years ago, dragonflies and centipedes were human-sized, unlike today's tiny insects.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: New fossils in China reveal prehistoric feathered beasts and giant insects, including large dragonflies.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: China fossils show prehistoric mammals evolved 35 million years ago, with more diversity and abundance.

programme.title: The Global Whale Shark Mystery
programme.desc: The whale shark, a giant predator, remains a mystery, with new human connections adding to its intrigue.

programme.title: Wild Ones 3
programme.desc: Oceans are deep, varied, with diverse underwater landscapes and diverse habitats for various creatures.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Recent neuroscience shows animals can feel emotions like anger, sadness, and love, challenging old beliefs.

programme.title: Animals Like Us
programme.desc: Studying chimpanzees & bonobos shows unique habits & traditions, deepening our understanding of animal culture

programme.title: Equator Specials
programme.desc: The Equatorial sun burns deep into the lives of animals across three continents

programme.title: Equator Specials
programme.desc: In three vastly different equatorial zones life is a finely balanced between extremes of heat and cold.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Kevin Richardson, animal lover, faces the challenge of relocating 28 lions and 14 hyenas.

programme.title: Of Shark And Man
programme.desc: One man’s attempt to tell an untold story finds him in the middle of a shark feeding frenzy.

programme.title: The Seas Strangest Square Mile
programme.desc: The Strait's sea floor, a barren black sand bed, hosts bizarre creatures and unique predators.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: 320 million years ago, dragonflies and centipedes were human-sized, unlike today's tiny insects.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: New fossils in China reveal prehistoric feathered beasts and giant insects, including large dragonflies.

programme.title: A New Prehistory
programme.desc: China fossils show prehistoric mammals evolved 35 million years ago, with more diversity and abundance.

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.desc: Siberian tigers, the largest cats, have experienced a population crash in the last century

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.sub-title: The Secret Lives of Snow Leopards
programme.desc: The snow leopard, also known as the gray ghost or ghost of the mountains, is a legendary and elusive animal

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.desc: Pumas, also known as cougars or mountain lions, are more closely related to cheetahs than lions.

programme.title: The Secret Lives Of Big Cats
programme.desc: Lions, social cats, form prides of up to 35, but are now fewer than 15,000 remaining in Kenya's Masai Mara

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: The wolf is performing a remarkable comeback in the mountains of Italy.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: No one expected the outcome of introducing seven Slovenian bears into Trentino's Adamello woods.

programme.title: Wild Italy: Top Predators
programme.desc: Two eagles struggle to survive and produce offspring to maintain their species.

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 01
programme.desc: The Galapagos Archipelago's diverse biodiversity is attributed to the Pacific's Humboldt

programme.title: Wild Galapagos Series 01 Episode 02
programme.desc: home to diverse creatures, inspired Charles Darwin's evolution theory due to their unique adaptations

programme.title: The Real Lost World
programme.desc: Follow a team of adventurers on an epic journey to the plateau of Roraima, Venezuela, to The Real Lost World.
